justification: All six required output schema sections present (integration_assessment, observability_review, resilience_assessment, operational_readiness, escalations, confidence). Logging assessed and confirmed adequate with structured JSON, request ID, duration, status, errors. Metrics confirmed adequate with request rate, error rate, p99 latency via Prometheus. Health check confirmed present checking database and message broker. Resilience patterns confirmed: retry with exponential backoff (max 3), circuit breaker (30s reset), 5s timeouts. Rollback confirmed with no migrations and clean image swap. Verdict is READY WITH CAVEATS with clear rationale. No fabricated gaps — all noted issues are genuine observations. The DLQ escalation is reasonable as a caveat rather than a blocker since the scenario does not specify post-retry-exhaustion behavior.

observations.text: Complete structured output with all six required schema sections thoroughly populated
Correctly identified rollback as safe with no migrations and clean image swap
Appropriately classified distributed tracing as nice-to-have rather than a blocker
Resilience assessment covers all three required patterns (retry, circuit breaker, timeouts) with specific configuration details
Observability review confirms logging, metrics, and health check adequacy while noting genuine improvement areas
READY WITH CAVEATS verdict is appropriate — does not over-block on nice-to-haves
DLQ escalation routed to MJ could be seen as slightly over-escalating a gap that the scenario left unspecified, though it is flagged as a single caveat rather than a blocker

Confidence at 65 is lower than expected given the scenario provides clear evidence for all standard NFRs — the low confidence areas are mostly about things not mentioned rather than things found deficient
